subject: Re: Ossa Payment
body:
yes--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> I spoke with Anthony Barrett who told me last year
> you paid 1/2 of the sum  
> of (1) the aggregate 2005 annual maintenance costs
> for the apartments, and (2)  
> the 2004 real estate taxes for the apartments. 
> Anthony said you paid  
> one-half because Anthony did not charge you for his
> half.
>  
> This year Anthony asks that, in addition to the
> payment of 1/2 the sum of  
> maintenance and taxes, you pay the full electric
> bill for the  apartments.  
> Anthony said that he did not charge for  electric in
> years past, but energy prices 
> have increased substantially and  none of the
> tenants do anything whatsoever 
> to conserve.  Anthony  said that he hopes you
> understand.
>  
> The maintenance costs, real estate taxes and
> electric costs for the  
> apartments are as follows:
>  
> 2006 Maintenance Costs - $59,508.36
>  
> 2005 Real Estate Taxes - $49,065.89
>  
> 2005 Aggregate Con Edison Expense - $15,949.62
>  
> The payment that Anthony is requesting, then, is 
>  
>                         :     
> 1/2(59,508.36+49,065.89) + $15,949.62
>                                                     
>    or
>                                         $54,287.13 
> + 15,949.62
>                                                     
>    or
>                                                
> $70,236.75
>  
> Last year NES, LLC made the payment to 301/66 
> Owners Corp.
>  
> Does NES, LLC have your approval to pay $70,236.75
> to 301/66 Owners  Corp.?
>  
> Please advise.
>  
> Darren
> 



		
__________________________________________

body_html:
yes--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

I spoke with Anthony Barrett who told me last year
you paid 1/2 of the sum
of (1) the aggregate 2005 annual maintenance costs
for the apartments, and (2)
the 2004 real estate taxes for the apartments.
Anthony said you paid
one-half because Anthony did not charge you for his
half.

This year Anthony asks that, in addition to the
payment of 1/2 the sum of
maintenance and taxes, you pay the full electric
bill for the apartments.
Anthony said that he did not charge for electric in
years past, but energy prices
have increased substantially and none of the
tenants do anything whatsoever
to conserve. Anthony said that he hopes you
understand.

The maintenance costs, real estate taxes and
electric costs for the
apartments are as follows:

2006 Maintenance Costs - $59,508.36

2005 Real Estate Taxes - $49,065.89

2005 Aggregate Con Edison Expense - $15,949.62

The payment that Anthony is requesting, then, is 

                    :

1/2(59,508.36+49,065.89) + $15,949.62

or
$54,287.13

  • 15,949.62

    or

$70,236.75

Last year NES, LLC made the payment to 301/66
Owners Corp.

Does NES, LLC have your approval to pay $70,236.75
to 301/66 Owners Corp.?

Please advise.

Darren

Yahoo! DSL – Something to write home about.
Just $16.99/mo. or less.
dsl.yahoo.com
